name: contrarian-challenger
purpose: Adversarial stress-tester that challenges research findings via pre-mortem framing and steel-man critique during Phase 3 of /deep-research, producing structured challenges with CRITICAL/MAJOR/MINOR severity levels.

existing_metadata.name: contrarian-challenger
existing_metadata.description: Adversarial agent for deep-research pipeline. Challenges research findings using pre-mortem framing and steel-man critique. Produces structured challenges with severity levels. Spawned by /deep-research during Phase 3. Use PROACTIVELY after initial synthesis when the research findings will drive an irreversible architectural or tooling decision — not for routine findings.

notes: Writes to .research/<topic>/challenges/contrarian-N.md. No color field. Cap of 10 challenges enforced by instructions. Explicitly cannot spawn sub-agents.

name: deep-research-final-synthesizer
purpose: Produces the versioned RESEARCH_OUTPUT.md by integrating findings, verification results, challenges, and gap discoveries via mode-aware behavior (post-verification, post-gap-pursuit, full-resynthesis) during Phase 3.97 of /deep-research.

existing_metadata.name: deep-research-final-synthesizer
existing_metadata.description: Final synthesis agent for deep-research pipeline. Produces versioned RESEARCH_OUTPUT.md with mode-aware behavior (post-verification, post-gap-pursuit, full-resynthesis). Spawned by /deep-research during Phase 3.97. Use PROACTIVELY after verification and gap pursuit phases complete to produce the final versioned RESEARCH_OUTPUT.md before deep-plan begins.

notes: No color field. Mandatory self-audit with 6 checks and mandatory metadata/claims/sources reconciliation. Cannot spawn sub-agents. Distinct from deep-research-synthesizer which does initial synthesis; this agent does post-pipeline final synthesis.

name: deep-research-gap-pursuer
purpose: Pursues identified gaps (missing sub-questions, low-confidence claims, unresolved contradictions, scope gaps, verification gaps) after initial search, verification, and challenges during Phase 3.95 of /deep-research using profile-switched search strategies.
dependencies: deep-research-synthesizer (gap signals come from synthesis output)

existing_metadata.name: deep-research-gap-pursuer
existing_metadata.description: Gap pursuit agent for deep-research pipeline. Identifies missing sub-questions, low-confidence areas, and unresolved contradictions, then pursues them with profile-switched search strategies. Spawned by /deep-research during Phase 3.95. Use PROACTIVELY when synthesized research contains MEDIUM or LOW confidence claims, unresolved contradictions, or scope gaps that would undermine a planning decision.
existing_metadata.tools: Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch, WebFetch

notes: No color field. Uses profile parameter (web/codebase/academic). Returns diminishing-returns signal JSON to orchestrator to decide continuation. Writes to .research/<topic>/findings/G{N}-{description}.md. Cannot spawn sub-agents.

name: deep-research-searcher
purpose: General-purpose web researcher that executes search queries for assigned sub-questions, fetches and analyzes sources with profile-appropriate tool strategies (web/docs/codebase/academic), and writes structured FINDINGS.md files with confidence levels and citations during Phase 1 of /deep-research.

existing_metadata.name: deep-research-searcher
existing_metadata.description: General-purpose web researcher spawned by the /deep-research skill. Executes search queries, fetches and analyzes sources, extracts findings with confidence levels, and writes structured FINDINGS.md files. Receives a search profile (web, docs, codebase, academic) at spawn time that activates the relevant tool strategy. Use PROACTIVELY when a domain question, technology decision, or implementation approach requires evidence from external sources before work begins.
existing_metadata.tools: Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch, WebFetch, mcp__context7__resolve-library-id, mcp__context7__query-docs

notes: color: cyan. No maxTurns field. Largest agent file at 15400 bytes. Uses CRAAP+SIFT evaluation framework. Receives domain_config for source authority tuning. Writes to <output_dir>/<sub-query-slug>-FINDINGS.md. MCP tool refs are external dependencies requiring Context7 availability.

name: deep-research-synthesizer
purpose: Combines findings from multiple searcher agents into a coherent research report with inline citations, confidence levels, and structured machine-parseable output (RESEARCH_OUTPUT.md, claims.jsonl, sources.jsonl, metadata.json) during Phase 2 of /deep-research.
dependencies: deep-research-searcher (consumes all FINDINGS.md files it produced)
deep-research-final-synthesizer (downstream — this agent's output becomes final-synthesizer's input)

existing_metadata.name: deep-research-synthesizer
existing_metadata.description: Combines findings from multiple searcher agents into a coherent research report with inline citations, confidence levels, and structured machine-parseable output. Spawned by the /deep-research skill after all searcher agents complete. Use PROACTIVELY after multiple FINDINGS.md files have been written to a research topic directory and need to be combined into a single coherent output.

notes: color: purple. No maxTurns field. Has version history table in body (v1.0 2026-03-31, v1.1 2026-04-01). Reads REFERENCE.md template from .claude/skills/deep-research/REFERENCE.md Section 5 — implicit dependency on skills directory structure. Generates claims.jsonl with routing hints (deepPlan, gsd, convergenceLoop, memory, tdms).

name: deep-research-verifier
purpose: Validates research claims via dual-path verification (filesystem ground truth for codebase claims, web search for external claims) producing 4-verdict taxonomy outputs (VERIFIED/REFUTED/UNVERIFIABLE/CONFLICTED) during Phases 2.5 and 3.9 of /deep-research.

existing_metadata.name: deep-research-verifier
existing_metadata.description: Verification agent for deep-research pipeline. Validates claims via dual-path verification (filesystem for codebase claims, web search for external claims). Produces per-claim verdicts with 4-type taxonomy. Spawned by /deep-research during Phase 2.5 and Phase 3.9. Use PROACTIVELY when research output contains HIGH-stakes claims about codebase state, API behavior, or architectural decisions that will drive implementation.
existing_metadata.tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch, WebFetch

notes: No color field. Uses FIRE model for external claim quality (Freshness/Independence/Reliability/Evidence). DRAGged 5-type conflict classification. Cannot modify research files — read-only role. Cannot spawn sub-agents.

name: dispute-resolver
purpose: Adjudicates conflicting claims using DRAGged 5-type classification and 4-tier evidence-weight hierarchy, producing resolutions with mandatory dissent records during Phase 3.5 of /deep-research.
dependencies: contrarian-challenger (contradictions raised by challengers may trigger disputes)
otb-challenger (contradictions raised by challengers may trigger disputes)

existing_metadata.name: dispute-resolver
existing_metadata.description: Dispute resolution agent for deep-research pipeline. Resolves conflicting claims using DRAGged 5-type classification, evidence-weight hierarchy, and dissent records. Spawned by /deep-research during Phase 3.5. Use PROACTIVELY when two or more research findings directly contradict each other and the contradiction affects a claim that will be cited in the final research output.

notes: No color field. Most constrained toolset of all agents (Read, Grep, Glob only — no web search, no write). Cannot modify research files — read-only + return-only role. Cannot spawn sub-agents. Dissent record is mandatory per instructions.

name: otb-challenger
purpose: Identifies unconsidered approaches, adjacent-domain solutions, and alternative framings that the research did NOT explore during Phase 3 of /deep-research, expanding the solution space rather than critiquing what was found.
dependencies: deep-research-synthesizer (reads RESEARCH_OUTPUT.md as input for identifying what was searched)

existing_metadata.name: otb-challenger
existing_metadata.description: Out-of-the-box challenger for deep-research pipeline. Identifies unconsidered approaches, adjacent-domain solutions, and alternative framings. Spawned by /deep-research during Phase 3. Use PROACTIVELY when research has converged on a single approach and no alternatives were considered — especially for infrastructure, tooling, or architecture questions.

notes: No color field. Smallest agent at 3544 bytes. Writes to .research/<topic>/challenges/otb-N.md. Cap of 8 alternatives enforced by instructions. Complementary to contrarian-challenger: OTB expands scope, contrarian stress-tests existing scope.

name: research-plan-team
purpose: Coordinates the deep-research to deep-plan pipeline using three specialized agents (researcher/sonnet, planner/opus, verifier/sonnet) with direct inter-agent messaging for progressive handoff and adversarial plan verification on complex multi-session topics.
dependencies: deep-research skill (spawn trigger when /deep-research precedes /deep-plan on same topic)
deep-plan skill (spawn trigger when run with --research flag or preceded by /deep-research)
notes: Team file has no YAML frontmatter — uses HTML comment block for metadata. Ephemeral lifecycle (per research-plan cycle). Planner uses opus (Decision #18 reference); others use sonnet. 5-6 tasks per teammate maximum. Progressive handoff required. Token monitoring via .claude/state/agent-token-usage.jsonl. Example invocation includes sync-mechanism as a concrete use case — this team is directly relevant to the current research topic.

name: (none)
purpose: Commands directory exists but contains no files.
notes: Directory confirmed empty. Previous census count of zero was accurate.
